ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #12, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-30-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Philip Robb appeared by zoom for and with Arina Fong (by zoom), Adora Fong (by zoom) and for Adea Fong (Co-Trustees).

The Court informed the parties that in the future they are not to dispose of any assets such as the van without prior Court authority, and stated that the request for compensation of the caregiver services provided by the Co-Trustees appears to be a fair amount and that the Paralegal fees might require a modest cut based on its review. Mr. Robb informed the Court that Mr. Ross has now retired. The Court granted the Twelfth Account and Report of Successor Co-Trustees and Petition for Its Settlement, etc. (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.

Successor Co-Trustees Petition For Instructions Regarding Purchase Of Wheelchair Accessible Van; And For Allowance Of Attorneys Fees (Probate Code 17200)
Off Calendar Feb-22-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Philip Robb for and with Adora Fong (Co-Trustee).

Mr. Robb stated that this request is to replace a van that is worn out, that the van they have is not reparable, that parts are not available and that the recommendation of the mechanic is to purchase a new van. Ms. Fong stated that the company they hired to repair the van suggested getting a new van, that this van is fourteen years old, that they have had issues with it in the last few years and spoke of the physical requirement on the family to assist their Sister. Ms. Fong also stated that she believes they can get a credit for it, but she does not know, and that they can put a factory made chair back in the van and sell it. Mr. Robb addressed his fee request stating that his initial research was to determine if the purchase of the van was in need of a petition, that he spent a little over three hours checking local rules and that the substitution of attorney was required. The Court granted the Petition for Instructions Regarding Purchase of Wheelchair Accessible Van, etc. (subject to review of the order).

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

PETITION RE SUBSTITUTED JUDGMENT TRUST
Off Calendar Aug-07-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sterling Ross for Petitioners.

The Court stated that there is a notice issue, that notice was sent prior to the petition being filed, and that there is a question with regard to specialty paralegal time being high. Mr. Sterling explained that the hearing was on June 20th, that the paralegal mailed the notice on May 17th and concurrently filed the amendment electronically, and that it was not accepted because his signature wasn't on it. Mr. Sterling also stated that the paralegal sent the revised petition on May 17th, but it actually got filed on May 18th, that 30-day notice was provided, that there is no prejudice to any party and asked the Court to waive the notice requirement. Furthermore, Mr. Sterling explained services provided, time spent responding to the Probate Examiner's notes, time spent addressing the issue of $34,000.00 being paid to improve the Beneficiary's home, stated that the house is not owned by the Trust, that it is owned by the Petitioners, that the Petitioners were not aware that Court approval was required, spoke of complicated caregiver fee issues, complicated issues due to the civil case number, fees that were paid in the last accounting, etc. to support his fee requests. The Court noted the importance of not sending notice until verification of documents being filed and stated that earlier accountings were not quite as high. The Eleventh Account and Report of Co-Trustees, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
